page: Microsoft Azure D1 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the D family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated physical core and vCPU utilizing the x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache. The system is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and includes 51 GB of local storage. No GPU accelerators are present in this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations place the performance of this instance in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics both register at 1012.89, falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show 90.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks, dropping to 11.37 GB/sec for large read blocks and 9.54 GB/sec for large write blocks. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show slightly better relative performance, scoring in the bottom 25% with 20.68 GB/sec and 8.05 GB/sec respectively. Geekbench testing yields a single-core score of 1015.0 and a multi-core score of 1035.0, reflecting the single-threaded limitation of the hardware.

From a resource density perspective, the D1 instance offers a low-cost entry point for basic computing tasks, though its performance-per-resource ratio is constrained by the single-core architecture. Passmark CPU and memory scores reside in the bottom 10%, with database operations scoring 522.43 and Redis SET operations reaching 1.51 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is limited to 1.47 GB/sec. Consequently, this server is suited for lightweight utility workloads, simple development or testing environments, basic text processing, and low-traffic web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D1 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with a single dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPU operating at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, 51 GB of local storage, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data indicates that this instance delivers entry-level performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory read bandwidth is measured at 11.37 GB/sec for large blocks, and Redis SET operations reach 1.51 million operations per second. Given these performance constraints, the D1 is positioned as a low-cost option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic development environments, low-traffic static web serving, simple text processing, and utility tasks that do not require high computational density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D1 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 1 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPU, 4.0 GB of memory, and 51 GB of local storage, designed for entry-level workloads and basic utility tasks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D1 is an entry-level general-purpose VM with 1 Intel Xeon vCPU, 4.0 GB RAM, and 51 GB local storage for basic utility workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, single-core virtual machine with dedicated compute resources and local storage for basic utility and development workloads.
bullet_points: Single dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPU at 2.6 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 51 GB of local storage
Entry-level performance profile suited for low-resource tasks
Low-density hardware configuration for cost-efficient utility hosting
Best suited for basic development, testing, and low-traffic web serving
